MIKE ROOS 
ASSEMBLYMAN, FORTY·SIXTH DISTRICT 
February 25, 1981 
Mr. Ted Prim, Executive Director 
Fair Political Practices Comraission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Dear Mr. Prim: 
COMMITTEES: 
VICE.CHAIRMAN 
HOUSING AND COMMUNITY 
DEVELOPMENT 
CRIMINAL JUSTICE 
WAYS AND MEANS 
SUBCOMMITTEES: 
CHAIRMAN 
HOUSING PRODUCTION 
VICE.CHAIRMAN 
JOINT COMMITTEE ON PEACE 
OFFICER CLASSIFICATION 
WAYS AND MEANS. 
SUBCOMMITTEE 4-STATE 
ADMINISTRATION 
SELECT COMMITTEE ON THE 
REVISION OF THE NONPROFIT 
CORPORATIONS CODE 
Last year Los Angeles City Council President John Ferraro and I 
started the Ferraro/Roos Ren·ter Advisory Committee for our mutual 
constituents. 
Presently, about 6,000 renters belong to the Com-
mittee. 
We solicit their views on landlord/tenant issues, keep 
them informed of changes in State and city laws and operate a 
recorded phone message which is updated every two weeks with 
renter information. 
The number has been disseminated to the 
general public. 
Both Councilman Ferraro and I have shared the costs of printing 
and mailing from our campaign funds. 
However, as the number of 
renters who join our Co~~ittee has grown so has the costs to our 
campaign funds. 
Therefore, we would like to make the Committee 
self-financing. 
Our plan is to ask each member of the Committee for donations of 
$1 or $2 dollars through a mail solicitation. 
The donations 
from the letter would be used to help defray the costs of the 
Renter Advisory Committee, and no funds would be used by the 
cwapaigns of either myself or Councilman Ferraro or any other 
political campaign. 
All COlnmittee members would receive a yearly 
accounting of how the funds collected were used and whether a deficit 
or surplus remains. 
A separate bank account under the Committee name 
would be established. 
~.a 


Mr. Ted Prim 
Page 2 
We would, however, like your opinion on the best way to proceed. 
Is it necessary that the Renter Advisory Committee file with the 
FPPC as a separate committee with its own ID number? 
Or may we 
simply open a separate bank account for the Committee and begin 
soliciting? If FPPC requirements do not regulate such an 
organization are there reporting requirements with any other 
agency? 
If you have questions about this request, please call Jim McDermott 
in my district office at (213) 386-8042. 
Thank you for your 
assistance. 
Leader 
MR:bjc

sections.question: Is it necessary that the Renter Advisory Committee file with the FPPC as a separate committee with its own ID number? Or may we simply open a separate bank account for the Committee and begin soliciting? If FPPC requirements do not regulate such an organization are there reporting requirements with any other agency?
sections.question_synthetic: Assemblyman Mike Roos and LA City Council President John Ferraro seek guidance on whether their jointly-operated Ferraro/Roos Renter Advisory Committee must register with the FPPC as a separate committee if they solicit member donations to make it self-financing, and whether other agencies have jurisdiction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document contains a clear question section but no conclusion/advice from FPPC. Document quality is poor (OCR artifacts present) but key content is extractable. No FPPC letterhead or response language present.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Is it necessary that the Renter Advisory Committee file with the FPPC as a separate committee with its own ID number? Or may we simply open a separate bank account for the Committee and begin soliciting? If FPPC requirements do not regulate such an organization are there reporting requirements with any other agency?

The number has been disseminated to the general public. Both Councilman Ferraro and I have shared the costs of printing and mailing from our campaign funds. embedding.summary: Assemblyman Mike Roos requests FPPC guidance on the regulatory and filing requirements for the Ferraro/Roos Renter Advisory Committee, a joint project with LA City Council President John Ferraro that currently has 6,000 members and is funded through campaign funds but seeks to become self-financing through member donations.
